measurements.label: Accurate retrieval
evidence: PASS: [1 — Accurate retrieval: exact, synonym, and distractor resistance] synonym retrieval finds breakfast preference
PASS: [1 — Accurate retrieval: exact, synonym, and distractor resistance] implicit preference recall affects answer style
PASS: [1 — Accurate retrieval: exact, synonym, and distractor resistance] retrieval not dominated by unrelated noise

measurements.label: Test-time learning / cross-session learning
evidence: PASS: [2 — Test-time learning and cross-session persistence] new fact can be saved during test run
PASS: [2 — Test-time learning and cross-session persistence] newly learned fact persists across chat sessions

measurements.label: Long-range understanding under filler turns
evidence: PASS: [3 — Long-range understanding across many turns] long-range same-session anchor survives distractor turns

measurements.label: Conflict resolution and selective forgetting
evidence: PASS: [4 — Temporal updates and stale fact suppression] correction/update turn succeeds
PASS: [5 — Selective forgetting and retention of neighbours] forget request returns without crash/misroute
FAIL: [5 — Selective forgetting and retention of neighbours] forgotten target is not returned by memory search
PASS: [5 — Selective forgetting and retention of neighbours] neighbour memory remains after selective forget

measurements.label: Information extraction from conversation
evidence: PASS: [10 — Episodic capture, redaction, and settings toggles] episodic user row captured with alphabetic sentinel

measurements.label: Multi-session reasoning
evidence: PASS: [2 — Test-time learning and cross-session persistence] new fact can be saved during test run
PASS: [2 — Test-time learning and cross-session persistence] newly learned fact persists across chat sessions

PASS: [8 — Multi-hop synthesis, not raw single-fact lookup] multi-hop answer is a synthesis, not just a raw list

measurements.label: Tool routing discipline
evidence: PASS: [5 — Selective forgetting and retention of neighbours] forget request returns without crash/misroute

benchmarkNotes: Scores are internal regression proxies, not official leaderboard scores for MemoryAgentBench, LongMemEval, LoCoMo, or Mem2ActBench.
A pass/fail assertion contributes full credit/zero credit; warnings contribute half credit because they usually indicate eventual success with timing or reliability degradation.
The weighted score deliberately gives extra weight to isolation, governance, conflict resolution, and retrieval because these are the most production-critical memory failure modes.
